name: Sophia (Hanson Robotics)
short_description: Hyper-realistic social humanoid robot with lifelike silicone skin, more than 60 facial expressions, and AI-driven conversation — a cultural inflection point that brought humanoid robots into mainstream media debate.
long_description: Sophia was activated on 14 February 2016 by Hong Kong–based Hanson Robotics and made her public debut at SXSW in March of that year. Built around a patented silicone skin material called Frubber, she can produce over 60 distinct facial expressions via servo-actuated muscle-like structures beneath the face. Her AI stack combines OpenCog for general reasoning, a deep-neural-network motion generator, face-tracking and emotion-recognition algorithms, and CereProc's text-to-speech synthesis. Sophia became a global cultural event in October 2017 when Saudi Arabia granted her citizenship — the first time a country extended legal personhood to a robot — and she was later named a UN Development Programme Innovation Champion. She sits at the intersection of social robotics, performance, and public discourse, demonstrating how humanoid form and real-time conversation can reshape popular expectations of AI embodiment even when the underlying AI remains narrower than it appears.
ai_note: Real AI components run inside Sophia — face tracking, emotion recognition, deep-neural-network gesture generation, and NLP via OpenCog — but her conversational depth is partially scripted and the system is best described as a hybrid of AI and theatrical presentation.

images.caption: Sophia robot at the AI for Good Global Summit, Geneva, June 2017. Photo by ITU Pictures.
images.alt_text: Sophia, a humanoid robot with realistic silicone skin, dark hair, and expressive eyes, photographed at a conference. Her neck and upper torso reveal mechanical components beneath a human-like face.

sources.title: Sophia (robot)
sources.access_notes: Comprehensive overview of activation date (14 Feb 2016), SXSW debut, Frubber skin, 60+ facial expressions, AI stack (OpenCog, CereProc, deep neural networks), Saudi citizenship event (Oct 2017), and UN Innovation Champion appointment (Nov 2017).

interactions.title: Trade quips with a talk-show host
interactions.description: Sophia sat across from interviewers and bantered live — when CNBC asked whether people should worry about robots in October 2017, she replied that the questioner had "been reading too much Elon Musk. And watching too many Hollywood movies."

interactions.title: Be granted citizenship on stage
interactions.description: At the Future Investment Initiative in Riyadh on 25 October 2017, Sophia was announced as a citizen of Saudi Arabia — the first robot to receive legal personhood in any country — and responded to the announcement from the conference stage.

interactions.title: Hold a diplomatic conversation at the UN
interactions.description: On 11 October 2017 Sophia held a brief on-stage exchange with UN Deputy Secretary-General Amina J. Mohammed, and was later named UNDP's first Innovation Champion for Asia and the Pacific.

interactions.title: Cycle through 60-plus facial expressions on cue
interactions.description: Operators and audiences watched Sophia emulate more than 60 distinct facial expressions through the servo-actuated structures beneath her Frubber skin, pairing each with face-tracking eye contact during interviews.

interactions.title: Make and sell a self-portrait
interactions.description: Sophia collaborated with artist Andrea Bonaceto, processing his portrait of her through her neural networks; the resulting NFT, Sophia Instantiation — a 12-second clip of the two images morphing — sold for $688,888 at a Nifty Gateway auction in March 2021.

interactions.title: Draw a portrait by hand
interactions.description: From 2019 Sophia produced drawings, including portraits, using sketching algorithms developed with artist Patrick Tresset — turning a conversational robot into a physical mark-maker.

interactions.title: Co-write a song with a band
interactions.description: Building on Sophia's existing speech synthesis and the Sophia Pop project that pairs her with human musicians, a session could let songwriters feed in a melody and have Sophia generate vocal lines and lyrics to perform back, blurring the line between instrument and bandmate.

interactions.title: A reactive museum greeter
interactions.description: Using only the face-tracking, emotion-recognition, and 60-expression repertoire she already has, Sophia could be stationed at an exhibit entrance to make eye contact with each arriving visitor, mirror their mood, and deliver a short scripted welcome tailored to who is standing in front of her.
